text: International collaboration in higher education has become a crucial issue for contemporary globalised world. On the one hand, universities in this era are needed to maintain their academic standards as well as to address the challenges faced by the world, hence the promotion of collaboration in higher education as a mean to promote mutual benefit in terms of education. Hence, identifying the challenges and opportunities of such collaboration is of particular importance. The purpose of this study is therefore aimed at identifying the challenges and opportunities of international collaboration in higher education institutions in the context of globalisation, and at shedding light on the complexities of such collaborative relations. To achieve the research objectives, both quantitative and qualitative data were collected through surveying the literature and by carrying out interviews with officials, as well as case studies of universities which have international relations in addition to comparative analysis of policies regulating international relations between higher education institutions in different systems. The study showed that the international relations between higher education institutions have its challenges and opportunities. These challenges include cultural differences; ensuring academic quality and the appropriate levels for the degree programmes offered, in addition to the logistical, financial and resources issues. On the other hand, such relations present opportunities for access to knowledge and sources, as well as diversity of visions and enhancing the level of research productivity and equipping students with the requirements of the global labour market. Thus, the collaboration between higher education institutions has several dimensions and requires precise planning, adequate communication and institutional coordination, in order to meet the challenges it entails, while benefiting from its opportunities in favour of universities, academics and students.
